branches.condition: Using manual Docker installation instead of 1-Click App
branches.effect_on_first_success: Requires more setup knowledge and time, but leads to same deployment success

branches.condition: Deploying database or service instead of application
branches.effect_on_first_success: First success milestone remains deployment of any resource

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: Start Deploying - Create your first project and deploy applications, databases, or services

documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: First application or service is deployed and accessible through Coolify with automatic SSL and domain configuration optionally set
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: Application status shows as deployed and accessible in Coolify dashboard; optionally domain resolves with SSL
documented_first_success.official_milestone: Successfully deploy first application or service and access it via Coolify web interface
documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: This milestone marks the transition from setup to actual deployment and usage of Coolify as intended, documented as the first success point in official docs.

assumed_prior_state: Developer has access to a server with SSH
Developer has basic knowledge of servers and SSH
Developer has a Git repository or Docker image for deployment
entry_point.developer_intent: Deploy an application or service on a self-hosted server using Coolify

friction_gates.description: Need to have SSH access to a server or VPS to install Coolify
Server must meet minimum hardware requirements (2 CPU cores, 2GB RAM recommended)
SSH key must be added to server for secure access
Create admin account with strong password for first login
DNS A record must be configured for custom domain to enable HTTPS

prerequisites.requirement: Have an account on your server or hosting provider with SSH access
Access to a server or VPS with minimum 2 CPU cores and 2GB RAM recommended
Docker installed or use Coolify 1-Click App which includes Docker
Basic understanding of SSH, Git repositories or Docker images for deployment

primary_path.action: Read introduction and prerequisites for Coolify self-hosting
details: Learn about server and SSH access needs
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Developer lacks server or SSH access

primary_path.action: Deploy Coolify on server using 1-Click App or manual Docker installation
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Server deployment or Docker installation fails
primary_path.input: Use DigitalOcean Marketplace or manual Docker commands
primary_path.output: Coolify platform installed and running on server
primary_path.success_signal: Server is accessible and Coolify service is running

primary_path.action: SSH into the server for initial setup
details: Run first login to trigger setup wizard
primary_path.failure_or_wait: SSH connection issues or setup errors
primary_path.output: Coolify setup wizard runs and localhost activated
primary_path.success_signal: Setup wizard completes and Coolify restarts

primary_path.action: Open Coolify web interface in browser
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Network or server issues prevent access

details: Fill registration form with username and strong password
First user gets full admin access
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Form validation errors or server errors
primary_path.output: Admin account created and logged in

primary_path.action: Create first project and add application or service resource
details: Connect Git repository or Docker image

primary_path.success_signal: Deployment status shows success and app accessible

primary_path.action: Optionally configure custom domain and SSL
details: Add DNS A record pointing to server IP
Update instance URL in Coolify settings
primary_path.failure_or_wait: DNS propagation delay or configuration errors
primary_path.success_signal: Application accessible via custom domain with SSL

sources.title: GitHub - coollabsio/coolify-docs: Documentation for Coolify · GitHub

sections_used: Creating an App using the Control Panel
sources.title: Coolify - DigitalOcean Marketplace Documentation

alternatives_considered.name: CLI
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: While SSH and CLI are used for initial server setup and activation, the main developer interaction surface for deployment and management is the web UI.

alternatives_considered.name: API
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: API exists for automation but is secondary to the web UI for first-mile onboarding and deployment.

surface.name: Web UI
surface.selection_basis: Coolify is primarily accessed and managed through a web interface for deploying and managing applications and services on self-hosted servers, as documented in official Coolify docs.
